title: catalog integrity and 71-biomarker hardening
summary: Corrected catalog metadata defects after the 71-biomarker expansion, preserved blank-unit urinalysis reference ranges, added regression coverage for the latest biomarker wave, and synced repo-facing notes with the current baseline.
completed: Corrected the BUN and Iron LOINC assignments
Preserved normalized reference ranges for unitless urinalysis biomarkers
Added regression tests for the 10 newest biomarkers and duplicate-LOINC detection
Updated the README to reflect 71 biomarkers and the 84.7% combined mapping rate
Advanced project memory to the hardened 71-biomarker checkpoint
decisions_locked: Catalog metadata changes now require explicit regression checks
Blank-unit reference ranges are valid for unitless biomarkers and should be preserved
Coverage expansions and repo-facing metadata updates should ship together
open_questions: When to re-run the 128K-row benchmark from the repaired baseline
Whether future catalog updates should validate terminology metadata against an external source
Which customer dataset should drive the next alias-expansion wave
next_steps: Re-run large-sample validation from the hardened baseline
Consider external terminology validation for future catalog updates
Continue alias expansion with customer/vendor datasets
resume_from: Start from project_memory/current_context.md. The repo now has corrected catalog metadata, preserved unitless urinalysis ranges, and regression coverage for the latest 71-biomarker baseline.
